title: Idea Schema
description: A consolidated knowledge unit extracted from a sheaf's MAP section. An Idea is an ε-state in the corpus's ε-machine: a set of claims (each with its MAP-selected variant) that together constitute one coherent piece of consolidated theory, together with the idea's own first-class scope, the consensus and frustration metrics internal to the idea, the ε-transitions that connect this idea to other ideas in the same sheaf, and the open questions — each decomposed into typed suggested_next_steps (experiment / simulation / theoretical_development / further_extraction / literature_review) — that residual frustration inside this idea feeds into. Ideas are the corpus's deliverable outputs — the things a reader browses, cites, or uses as the basis for follow-up work. Each Idea references its parent Sheaf so the full provenance chain (paper → claim → variant → restriction map → MAP section → idea) is traceable. One Sheaf produces N Ideas where N is the ε-state partition size (typically 3–10 for a corpus of a dozen or so claims). v0.2: replaced the free-form `proposed_tiebreaker` string with a structured `suggested_next_steps` array so downstream tools (visualizers, research-priority dashboards) can filter and rank work-to-do by type and effort.

idea_id.description: Unique identifier for this idea. Of the form `{corpus_tag}/idea_{ordinal}_{short_label}`. E.g. 'pendulum_corpus/idea_01_period_amplitude_coupling'.

label.description: Short human-readable label for the idea. E.g. 'Period increases with amplitude at second order in θ' or 'Damping suppresses amplitude-period coupling below critical Q'. Should be parseable as a claim on its own.

description.description: The consolidated statement this idea represents — typically a 2–4 sentence paraphrase that integrates the contributing claims' content, expressed in the scope the idea's contributing claims collectively support (not the scope any individual claim originally asserted).

sheaf_ref.description: Back-pointer to the sheaf this idea was extracted from. Preserves the full provenance chain; if the sheaf is re-computed at a different λ, ideas derived from the old section should be re-derived or marked stale.

sheaf_id.description: The sheaf_id field of the parent sheaf.

section_label.description: Which MAP section this idea came from — typically 'primary' (the stored winner), or a rank-N label if the idea was extracted from an alternative section for comparison. E.g. 'primary' or 'alt_section_rank_2'.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this idea was extracted from the sheaf. Used to detect staleness when the sheaf is re-computed.

contributing_claims.description: The claims whose MAP-selected variants together constitute this idea. Each entry carries the full reference chain: claim_id → selected variant → paper → credibility → rewrite distance. Order SHOULD be by descending credibility so the most load-bearing claim appears first.

selected_variant_id.description: The variant_id this idea inherits from the sheaf's MAP section. E.g. 'Liekhus2026:02#alt_small_angle'. If '#original', the claim contributed as-stated; if a rewrite, the idea inherits the rewritten scope.

paper_id.description: Paper this claim came from. E.g. 'Liekhus2026'.

credibility.description: The claim's credibility_score from its claim JSON. Cached here to avoid loading the full claim for display purposes.

rewrite_distance.description: Rewrite distance of the selected variant from the original. 0 means the claim contributed as originally stated.

role_in_idea.description: How this claim functions within this idea. 'primary' = a load-bearing claim the idea's description directly paraphrases. 'supporting' = an intermediate step or derivation the primaries rest on. 'observation' = an empirical input. 'caveat' = a boundary or scope-restriction claim that the idea inherits.

scope.description: First-class scope of this idea — the meet of its contributing claims' `scope.evidenced` blocks, using the same semilattice meet rules as the paper schema. This is the regime the idea is actually supported in, not the regime any individual paper asserted. Stored as its own field (not derived at read time) so ideas can be filtered and compared by regime without loading every contributing claim. A `derivation_timestamp` is stored so staleness can be detected when contributing claims are re-extracted.

generality.description: How broadly the consolidated idea is supported. Typically the most restrictive of the contributing claims' evidenced generalities, since the idea inherits scope narrowings from rewrites.

framework.description: Theoretical or computational framework the idea holds within. Meet of contributing frameworks. E.g. 'nonlinear equation of motion, amplitudes θ ≤ 15°, small damping'.

conditions.description: Intersection of contributing claims' `scope.evidenced.conditions`. E.g. ['planar motion', 'θ ≤ 15°', 'pivot friction negligible', 'Q > 10'].

derived_from_claims.description: List of contributing claim_ids whose scopes were met to produce this scope. Traceability field.

derivation_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when scope was computed from the contributing claims. If any contributing claim has been re-extracted since this timestamp, the idea's scope may be stale.

consensus.description: How well the contributing claims agree with each other on this idea. Summarizes the positive-side structure of the MAP section restricted to this idea.

n_papers_represented.description: Number of distinct papers contributing at least one claim to this idea. Ideas supported by multiple independent papers are more robust.

agreement_score.description: Mean of compatibility_scores on the restriction-map edges that lie entirely inside this idea (both endpoints are contributing claims). Positive values mean the idea's internal pairs agree; values near zero mean the claims are compatible but orthogonal; negative values indicate residual tension even within the idea (flagged via `frustration.residual_negative_edges`).

all_originals.description: True iff every contributing claim's selected_variant_id is an `#original`. False means at least one claim was rewritten to fit this idea — the idea depends on the sheaf's rewriting, not just the papers as-stated.

n_rewritten.description: Count of contributing claims whose selected variant is not the `#original`.

total_rewrite_cost.description: Sum of rewrite_distance across contributing claims. A proxy for how much scope-narrowing was required to make this idea cohere.

frustration.description: Residual disagreement within this idea. Most well-consolidated ideas have frustration.rho = 0 — tension that survives the MAP section typically sits on cross-idea edges, not intra-idea. An idea with rho > 0 is either (a) a cluster that should be split into two or more ideas, or (b) a genuinely contested region of consolidated theory worth surfacing to the reader.

rho.description: n_penrose / n_signed_triangles restricted to triangles whose three vertices are all contributing claims.

residual_negative_edges.description: Restriction-map edges entirely inside this idea whose compatibility_score on the MAP-selected variant pair is ≤ 0. These are the specific pairs that the sheaf could not fully resolve even with rewriting.

transitions_out.description: ε-transitions from this idea to other ideas in the same sheaf. Each transition describes how this idea feeds into, supplies, or is critiqued by another idea. Transitions are directional — put symmetric relationships in both directions.

kind.description: 'tool_supply' = this idea provides analytical/computational machinery the downstream idea uses. 'empirical_phenomenon' = this idea establishes a phenomenon the downstream idea explains. 'tool_under_critique' = this idea deploys a framework the downstream idea finds inadequate. 'trust_scaffolding' = this idea validates a code or method the downstream idea relies on. 'critique_of_framework' = this idea challenges a framework the downstream idea implicitly uses. 'extension' = the downstream idea generalizes or extends this one. 'specialization' = the downstream idea specializes this one to a narrower regime.

note.description: Short prose on why this transition holds.

supporting_edges.description: Restriction-map edge_ids that cross the two ideas and motivate this transition (positive-scored edges that structurally link the ideas).

transitions_in.description: ε-transitions into this idea from other ideas. Symmetric counterpart to transitions_out. Redundant for storage (derivable by inverting every transition_out in the corpus) but included for navigation convenience.

open_questions.description: Questions feeding from this idea's residual frustration or from ε-transitions pointing to under-developed downstream ideas. Each question cites the structural feature that motivates it and proposes a concrete tiebreaker — an experiment, calculation, or extraction that would resolve the residual.

question.description: The open question, phrased as a scientific or methodological query. E.g. 'Does the T₀(1 + θ²/16) second-order amplitude correction hold above θ = 45°?'

feeds_from.description: What structural feature inside the sheaf motivates this question.

residual_edge_ids.description: Edge_ids from residual_negative_edges that this question is tied to.

transition_pointers.description: Idea_ids of transition targets where the downstream idea is incomplete.

scope_gap.description: If the question reflects a scope gap (the idea holds in regime X but is asserted in regime Y), a prose description of the gap.

suggested_next_steps.description: Typed, actionable resolution strategies. Each entry is one concrete piece of work a researcher could undertake to resolve the question. Replaces v0.1's single `proposed_tiebreaker` string. Listing multiple steps per question is common — an experiment and a theoretical calculation may both contribute — and the `kind` enum lets the visualizer group them (e.g. 'show me all experimental next steps across the corpus' or 'which Ideas need theoretical development?').

kind.description: The category of work this step represents. 'experiment' = laboratory measurement or observation. 'simulation' = computational run (new code, new parameters, or new physics model). 'theoretical_development' = derivation, analytical calculation, proof. 'further_extraction' = extract additional claims from papers already in the corpus, or add new papers. 'literature_review' = survey related work beyond the corpus. 'code_capability' = build or extend a simulation code or analysis tool needed for the simulation. 'instrumentation' = develop a diagnostic or measurement capability needed for the experiment.

description.description: Concrete description of the step, specific enough to guide the next person picking it up. E.g. 'Measure period vs amplitude for a 30cm rigid pendulum at θ = 15°, 30°, 45°, 60°; fit to T₀(1 + aθ² + bθ⁴); check whether a matches 1/16.'

why_it_resolves.description: Short prose on what the step would establish, and which residual edge, scope gap, or transition target it bears on.

required_capabilities.description: What is needed to execute the step — equipment, codes, theoretical tools, data. E.g. ['rigid pendulum with adjustable length', 'high-speed camera at ≥ 500 fps', 'nonlinear-fit code with specified model'].

expected_outcome.description: What a successful result looks like. Specific enough that an observer can tell whether the step succeeded. E.g. 'Measured a coefficient consistent with 1/16 at ± 20%, across the full amplitude range.'

effort.description: Rough effort estimate. 'low' = days-to-weeks. 'medium' = weeks-to-months. 'high' = many months / new capability. 'programmatic' = multi-year or requires institutional commitment.

maturity.description: Whether the step is executable now with current capabilities, requires some tool/code/instrumentation to be developed first, or depends on another step being completed.

depends_on_step_ids.description: If `maturity` = 'depends_on_other_step', the step ids of prerequisite steps (within this Idea or via `cross_idea_refs` in another Idea).

priority.description: Relative importance of this question for advancing the idea — 'high' if the idea cannot be acted on without resolving it, 'low' if it's a refinement.

extraction.description: Provenance for how this idea was extracted from the sheaf.

method.description: 'epsilon_machine_partition' = computed by causal-state partition over the MAP section. 'llm_single' = LLM read the section and proposed the consolidation. 'hybrid' = LLM-proposed, human-verified. 'manual' = hand-specified by a domain expert.

human_reviewed.description: Whether a domain expert has reviewed this idea's label, description, scope, and transitions. High-stakes ideas SHOULD be reviewed before publication.
